summary:
Trabectedin (CMB305) has demonstrated clinically meaningful benefits in treating liposarcoma and leiomyosarcoma, especially in patients progressing after standard therapies. It is approved in both the U.S. and Europe as a second-line option based on improved progression-free survival and clinical benefit outcomes. 

The summary above contains redundant information about approval status and outcome metrics which are already covered elsewhere; therefore it should not appear again.

Final Summary:

Trabectedin (CMB305) shows proven effectiveness in lipid sarcoma and leiomyosarcoma when used post-anthracycline failure. Its regulatory recognition in major markets confirms its role as an effective second-line therapeutic for select sarcoma subtypes.

Trabectedin (CMB305) demonstrates clinical efficacy in treating liposarcoma and leiomyosarcoma, particularly among patients whose disease progresses after anthracycline-based therapy. Approved in the United States and European Union, it provides superior progression-free survival and clinical benefit compared to placebo/standard care in these indications.
